evidence: Disclosed: 1,093 (Q4FY26) vs. stated 2,000+ ceiling (55% utilization); ~150-200 orders below the ~1,225-1,300 analyst breakeven

hypothesis: Loss concentrated in a minority of stores
evidence: Disclosed: only ~25% of stores profitable as of Q2 FY26

hypothesis: New stores take 6-12mo to mature, dragging blended margin
evidence: No public store-cohort/vintage data available (analyst benchmark: Blinkit 3-6mo, non-metro 6-12mo)

evidence: Qualitative only — SKU count, ~50% of net adds

hypothesis: Density alone explains the margin gap across companies
evidence: Zepto: highest density (~2,045), near-worst loss. Blinkit: mid density (~1,425), best loss by far — business model likely dominates

hypothesis: Density predicts margin within a single network
verdict: Methodology demonstrated on calibrated proxy data
evidence: R²=0.42 on n=1143 simulated stores; proxy breakeven ≈ 1,619 (vs real-data ~1,225-1,300; slope agrees)
